alexander-dax.text: Dax Cornell Alexander (Democratic) is a candidate for U.S. Representative, TX-01.
As of 2026-07-04, FEC records show Dax Cornell Alexander had raised $12,467 and spent $10,893 for this campaign.
No public voting record in our set for Dax Cornell Alexander (not currently a member of Congress).

moran-nathaniel.text: Nathaniel Quentin Moran (Republican) is the current officeholder running for U.S. Representative, TX-01.
No public FEC campaign finance data in our set for Nathaniel Quentin Moran.

On 7-Jan-2025, Nathaniel Quentin Moran voted Yea on H R 29. A Yea vote required federal detention of unauthorized immigrants charged with theft, burglary, larceny, or assaulting a law enforcement officer, and let state attorneys general sue the federal government over certain immigration enforcement decisions.

base.summary: This is the U.S. House race for TX-01. In the most recent election (2024-11-05), Nathaniel Moran (REP) won by about 100.0 points out of 258523 votes cast. The bullets below summarize each current candidate's public finance and voting-record data where available.
base.caveats: This summary only includes facts found in our sourced dataset (FEC finance filings and U.S. House Clerk roll-call votes). It omits anything we could not verify from those sources. No public data in our set on any candidate's policy positions for this race (the positions field was empty for every candidate as of this build). No public data in our set on Dax Cornell Alexander's policy positions. No public data in our set on Nathaniel Quentin Moran's policy positions.
